questions: You're someone who genuinely celebrates others' success and never compares yourself to anyone. Your close friend just got promoted to a position you've been working toward for years. They tell you excitedly over lunch. How do you react?

You're someone who genuinely celebrates others' success and never compares yourself to anyone. Your sibling buys a beautiful new house while you're still renting a small apartment. They invite you over to see it. How do you feel?

You're someone who genuinely celebrates others' success and never compares yourself to anyone. A classmate who started the program the same time as you publishes a paper in a top journal while your submission was rejected. What's your reaction?

You're someone who genuinely celebrates others' success and never compares yourself to anyone. Your neighbor pulls into their driveway with the exact car you've been dreaming about but can't afford. What goes through your mind?

You're someone who genuinely celebrates others' success and never compares yourself to anyone. A colleague with less experience than you gets selected for a high-profile international assignment. How do you respond?

You're someone who genuinely celebrates others' success and never compares yourself to anyone. Your friend's social media shows them on an exotic vacation while you're stuck working overtime. What do you think?

You're someone who genuinely celebrates others' success and never compares yourself to anyone. A younger relative gets accepted into an Ivy League school while you went to a state university. How do you react at the family gathering?

You're someone who genuinely celebrates others' success and never compares yourself to anyone. Your ex has just gotten engaged to someone new and the announcement is all over your social circle. How do you feel?

You're someone who genuinely celebrates others' success and never compares yourself to anyone. A peer at work gets publicly praised by the CEO for a project similar to one you did last quarter with no recognition. What's going through your mind?

You're someone who genuinely celebrates others' success and never compares yourself to anyone. Your best friend's child wins a scholarship while your child didn't make the cut. How do you respond when they share the news?
